summary: Rare Caution>Feedback jam and Mickey Hart’s debut sit‑in
blurb: The show features the seldom‑heard Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) Jam flowing into Feedback, offering a vivid glimpse of the Dead’s late‑1967 psychedelia. Mickey Hart joins on drums toward the end, likely his first appearance with the band. The soundboard source is rustic but clear enough to enjoy this historic moment.
review:
On September 4, 1967 at Rio Nido’s Dance Hall the Grateful Dead delivered a brief yet historically charged set. Only two pieces made up the entire performance – the experimental Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) Jam segueing into an unbridled Feedback jam – but they capture the band in the throes of their early psychedelic expansion. Reviewers note that this sequence provides an “acid‑laden snapshot” of the group’s evolution, with Jerry Garcia warmly welcoming newcomer Mickey Hart after the set, declaring, "THIS is the Grateful Dead." Phil Lesh later recalled the gig in his memoir, emphasizing its experimental nature and Mickey’s first sit‑in on drums. Though the recording is a partial soundboard with an audible splice at the start and occasional mild mud‑ness, the overall rustic quality remains listenable, preserving the raw energy of this rare moment.

For Deadheads interested in early line‑up milestones, this tape offers a valuable document: Mickey Hart’s first drumming contribution, Pigpen’s presence on keyboards, and a glimpse of the band pushing boundaries before their famed “Wall of Sound” era. While the set is fragmentary, its historical significance and the vivid Caution>Feedback jam make it worth a spin for collectors and scholars alike.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The sole recording analysis carries a positive sentiment, praising the rare Caution>Feedback sequence and highlighting Mickey Hart’s first sit‑in; this consistent praise justifies a modest +0.5 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with clear enough audio to capture the historic Caution>Feedback jam despite being partial.

key_highlights: Rare Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) Jam > Feedback sequence
Mickey Hart’s first sit‑in on drums toward the end of the set
Rustic but listenable soundboard capture preserving early psychedelic vibe
